questions.question: The ancient Indian university of Taxila was a major center of learning for which of the following?
questions.explanation: The ancient Indian university of Taxila was a major center of learning for various subjects including Buddhism and Hinduism.

questions.question: Who was the founder of the Bahmani Kingdom in the Deccan region of India?
questions.explanation: Alauddin Hasan Bahman Shah was the founder of the Bahmani Kingdom in 1347.

questions.question: The Non-Cooperation Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi in the year
questions.explanation: The Non-Cooperation Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi in 1920.

questions.question: The Indus Valley Civilization is known for its sophisticated
questions.explanation: The Indus Valley Civilization is known for its sophisticated urban planning and architecture, agricultural practices, and trade networks.

questions.question: The Vijayanagara Empire was founded by
questions.explanation: The Vijayanagara Empire was founded by Harihara and Bukka Raya in 1336.

questions.question: The Permanent Settlement of 1793 was introduced by
questions.explanation: The Permanent Settlement of 1793 was introduced by Lord Cornwallis.

questions.question: The Quit India Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi in
questions.explanation: The Quit India Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi on August 8, 1942.

questions.question: The ancient Indian text, the Arthashastra, was written by
questions.explanation: The ancient Indian text, the Arthashastra, was written by Kautilya.

questions.question: The Sufi saint, Khwaja Moinuddin Chishti, was a contemporary of
questions.explanation: The Sufi saint, Khwaja Moinuddin Chishti, was a contemporary of Qutb-ud-din Aibak.

questions.question: The Indian National Congress was founded in
questions.explanation: The Indian National Congress was founded in 1885.

questions.question: What is the largest delta in India?
questions.explanation: The Ganges-Brahmaputra delta is the largest delta in India and one of the largest in the world.

questions.question: Which mountain range runs along the border between India and Myanmar?
questions.explanation: The Purvanchal range runs along the border between India and Myanmar.

questions.question: Which of the following is the largest iron ore producing state in India?
questions.explanation: Odisha is the largest iron ore producing state in India.

questions.question: What is the name of the cyclone that affected India in 2020, particularly the states of West Bengal and Odisha?
questions.explanation: Cyclone Amphan affected India in 2020, particularly the states of West Bengal and Odisha.

questions.question: Which river is known as the 'Ganga of the South'?
questions.explanation: The Godavari river is known as the 'Ganga of the South'.

questions.question: Which state in India is the largest producer of manganese?
questions.explanation: Odisha is the largest producer of manganese in India.

questions.question: What is the name of the strait that separates the islands of Little Andaman and Great Nicobar?
questions.explanation: The Ten Degree Channel separates the islands of Little Andaman and Great Nicobar.

questions.question: Which of the following ports is the largest container handling port in India?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru Port is the largest container handling port in India.

questions.question: Which of the following types of winds brings rainfall to the west coast of India during the summer months?
questions.explanation: The southwest monsoon winds bring rainfall to the west coast of India during the summer months.

questions.question: Which river flows through the states of Madhya Pradesh, Gujarat, and Rajasthan before emptying into the Arabian Sea?
questions.explanation: The Narmada river flows through the states of Madhya Pradesh, Gujarat, and Rajasthan before emptying into the Arabian Sea.

questions.question: What is the primary function of the Election Commission of India?
options: To conduct elections to the Parliament and State Legislatures
To oversee the judicial appointments in India
To advise the President on matters of national importance
To investigate cases of human rights violations
questions.explanation: The Election Commission of India is responsible for conducting free and fair elections to the Parliament and State Legislatures.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution provides for the Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression?
questions.explanation: Article 19 of the Indian Constitution provides for the Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression.

questions.question: What is the term of office for a member of the Rajya Sabha?
questions.explanation: The term of office for a member of the Rajya Sabha is 6 years.

questions.question: Who is the head of the Panchayati Raj system at the district level?
questions.explanation: The Zila Parishad Chairman is the head of the Panchayati Raj system at the district level.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a Fundamental Right guaranteed by the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Right to Property is not a Fundamental Right guaranteed by the Indian Constitution, although it is a Constitutional right under Article 300A.

questions.question: What is the minimum age for a person to be eligible to contest elections to the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: The minimum age for a person to be eligible to contest elections to the Lok Sabha is 25 years.

questions.question: Who is the final interpreter of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court of India is the final interpreter of the Indian Constitution.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the provisions related to the impeachment of the President?
questions.explanation: Article 61 of the Indian Constitution deals with the provisions related to the impeachment of the President.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 73rd and 74th Constitutional Amendments?
options: To establish a federal system of government
To strengthen the Panchayati Raj system
To promote the development of urban areas
To increase the number of seats in the Parliament
questions.explanation: The 73rd and 74th Constitutional Amendments aim to strengthen the Panchayati Raj system and urban local bodies respectively.

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of the Indian federal system?
options: A dual system of government with a clear separation of powers
A system of government where the central government has absolute power
A system of government where the states have no autonomy
questions.explanation: India has a single, unified judiciary which is a feature of the Indian federal system.
